topic: Two coworkers discover they went to the same summer camp
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara practically bounced in her chair, a wide, bright smile stretching across her face as she stared at the faded photograph. "No way! You were at Camp Whispering Pines too?" she exclaimed, her voice pitched high with genuine surprise. She tapped the glossy paper with a fingertip, her eyes sparkling with undisguised delight. The memory of that week—the sticky s'mores, the endless canoeing, the shared, breathless laughter—flooded her senses. "I thought I was the only one who survived the archery competition!" she chirped, throwing her hands up in a gesture of pure, uncontained joy.

Mark, across the desk, slowly lowered his coffee mug, a slow grin spreading across his features. He leaned back, letting out a low chuckle that vibrated in his chest. "Seriously? You remember the mud on your boots? I thought that was just a shared hallucination." He shook his head, his eyes crinkling at the corners as he took in her animated retelling. "It’s incredible, Elara. We actually shared that entire summer."

[story 2]

"You won't believe this!" Chloe practically vibrated with energy, pacing the small breakroom. Her hands were fluttering near her mouth, and her eyes darted around the office as if searching for confirmation of the impossible. "I just found this old brochure! It has the logo for Camp Redwood Creek! And guess who else is listed? You!" A bubbling sound escaped her throat, a sound bordering on a squeal of pure excitement. She spun around, her posture radiating kinetic energy, clearly unable to contain the sheer delight bubbling up inside her.

Liam, who had been quietly reading a technical manual, looked up, a slow, appreciative smile spreading across his face. He set his book down deliberately, his gaze warm and amused. "Well, look at you," he said, his tone laced with gentle humor. "You look like you just won the lottery. Tell me everything. Did you manage to keep that ridiculous friendship bracelet you made?"

[story 3]

"Oh my gosh, Mark! You are kidding me!" Sarah practically leaped from her seat, her entire body vibrating with restless motion. She was practically humming a tune, her eyes wide and shining as she gestured wildly toward the shared calendar. "We went to the same place! Camp Evergreen! I can’t believe it! We were there together for three whole weeks!" Her breath hitched slightly with the sheer magnitude of the coincidence. She started nodding rapidly, a bright, unstoppable smile pulling at the corners of her mouth. "Remember that time we tried to build that ridiculously tall fort? I still think about it!"
